patch.value: GH-603: history-repair cements cross-author data loss after a lossy merge — reverting patch discards the other author's mutation, validate fully green

history-repair surfaces an explicit revert/data-loss warning naming the discarded authors' events when re-anchoring discards effective-state mutations; validate or health exposes a post-repair divergence signal instead of full green; documented recovery path to re-apply discarded mutations; repro from GH-603 becomes a regression test

author: maintainer-agent
message: link verify-gate sibling: strict-exit gate is the detection half of the repair-loss loop

patch.value: GH-603. After a lossy merge resolution (.toon taken from one side, history union-merged), pm history-repair re-anchors by appending a reverting patch that discards the other author's mutation from effective state; validate then reports drift 0 / ok:true. A teammate agent's change is reverted invisibly — data loss is cemented. Fix direction: repair must emit an explicit revert warning naming discarded events/authors, expose a post-repair divergence signal in validate/health, and document a re-application recovery path. Detection half = pm-ol3p strict verify gate.
